title: Generating datastore checkpoints
abstract: The present disclosure is directed to generating datastore checkpoints. In particular, the methods and systems of the present disclosure may generate, within a datastore, data representing multiple checkpoints. Each checkpoint of the checkpoints may correspond to a respective record of the datastore and may represent a common shared value for a field based at least in part on which the datastore is ordered. Based at least in part on the checkpoints, the datastore may be queried to produce one or more responsive records to one or more criteria of the query. Based at least in part on the responsive record(s), training data may be generated. The training data may be utilized for training one or more machine learning (ML) models configured to process input based at least in part on values for the field based at least in part on which the datastore is ordered.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A method comprising: generating, by one or more computing devices and within a datastore, data representing a plurality of checkpoints, each checkpoint of the plurality of checkpoints corresponding to a respective record of the datastore and representing a common shared value for a field based at least in part on which the datastore is ordered; querying, by the one or more computing devices and based at least in part on the data representing the plurality of checkpoints, the datastore to produce one or more responsive records to one or more criteria of the query; generating, by the one or more computing devices and based at least in part on the one or more responsive records, training data for training one or more machine learning (ML) models configured to process input based at least in part on values for the field based at least in part on which the datastore is ordered; and storing, by the one or more computing devices, the training data for training the one or more ML models configured to process input based at least in part on values for the field based at least in part on which the datastore is ordered. 2. The method of claim 1, comprising generating, by the one or more computing devices and based at least in part on the training data, the one or more ML models configured to process input based at least in part on the values for the field based at least in part on which the datastore is ordered. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein: the field based at least in part on which the datastore is ordered is time; and the common shared value represents a common point in time. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein generating the data representing the plurality of checkpoints comprises: identifying one or more records of the datastore that do not share the common value for the field based at least in part on which the datastore is ordered; and for each record of the one or more records, creating a new record in the datastore indicating: the common shared value for the field based at least in part on which the datastore is ordered, and 

 at least one value for at least one other field that is different and distinct from the field based at least in part on which the datastore is ordered. 5. The method of claim 4, comprising, for each record of the one or more records, copying to the new record the at least one value for the at least one other field from the at least one other field for the record. 6. The method of claim 4, wherein generating the data representing the plurality of checkpoints comprises, for each record of the one or more records, determining the at least one value for the at least one other field based at least in part on performing an associative accumulation operation on one or more other values for the at least one other field associated with one or more other records of the datastore. 7. The method of clam 6, wherein generating the data representing the plurality of checkpoints comprises, for each record of the one or more records, identifying the one or more other records of the datastore based at least in part on their comprising data representing one or more previously generated checkpoints. 8. The method of claim 4, wherein generating the data representing the plurality of checkpoints comprises, for each record of the one or more records, determining the at least one value for the at least one other field based at least in part on performing an idempotent overwrite operation on one or more other values for the at least one other field associated with one or more other records of the datastore. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein generating the data representing the plurality of checkpoints comprises, for each record of the one or more records, identifying the one or more other records of the datastore based at least in part on their comprising data representing one or more previously generated checkpoints. 10. The method of claim 4, comprising, for each record of the one or more records, copying to the new record at least one value for each of a plurality of different and distinct other fields of the record. 11. The method of claim 4, comprising, for each record of the one or more records, selecting a subset of a plurality of different and distinct other fields of the record to be copied to the new record. 12. The method of claim 1, comprising purging, by the one or more computing devices and based at least in part on the data representing the plurality of checkpoints, a plurality of different and distinct records of the datastore. 13. A system comprising: one or more processors; and a memory storing instructions that when executed by the one or more processors cause the system to perform operations comprising: generating, within a datastore, data representing a plurality of checkpoints, each checkpoint of the plurality of checkpoints corresponding to a respective record of the datastore and representing a common shared value for a field based at least in part on which the datastore is ordered; and generating, based at least in part of the data representing the plurality of checkpoints, training data for one or more machine learning (ML) models configured to process input based at least in part on values for the field based at least in part on which the datastore is ordered. 14. The system of claim 13, wherein generating the data representing the plurality of checkpoints comprises: identifying one or more records of the datastore that do not share the common value for the field based at least in part on which the datastore is ordered; and for each record of the one or more records, creating a new record in the datastore indicating: the common shared value for the field based at least in part on which the datastore is ordered, and at least one value for at least one other field that is different and distinct from the field based at least in part on which the datastore is ordered. 15. The system of claim 14, wherein generating the data representing the plurality of checkpoints comprises, for each record of the one or more records, determining the at least 

one value for the at least one other field based at least in part on performing an associative accumulation operation on one or more other values for the at least one other field associated with one or more other records of the datastore. 16. The system of claim 14, wherein generating the data representing the plurality of checkpoints comprises, for each record of the one or more records, determining the at least one value for the at least one other field based at least in part on performing an idempotent overwrite operation on one or more other values for the at least one other field associated with one or more other records of the datastore. 17. One or more non-transitory computer-readable media comprising instructions that when executed by one or more computing devices cause the one or more computing devices to perform operations comprising generating, within a datastore, data representing a plurality of checkpoints, each checkpoint of the plurality of checkpoints corresponding to a respective record of the datastore and representing a common shared value for a field based at least in part on which the datastore is ordered. 18. The one or more non-transitory computer-readable media of claim 17, wherein generating the data representing the plurality of checkpoints comprises: identifying one or more records of the datastore that do not share the common value for the field based at least in part on which the datastore is ordered; and for each record of the one or more records, creating a new record in the datastore indicating: the common shared value for the field based at least in part on which the datastore is ordered, and at least one value for at least one other field that is different and distinct from the field based at least in part on which the datastore is ordered. 19. The one or more non-transitory computer-readable media of claim 18, wherein generating the data representing the plurality of checkpoints comprises, for each record of the one or more records, determining the at least one value for the at least one other field based at least in part on performing an associative accumulation operation on one or more other values for the at least one other field associated with one or more other records of the datastore. 20. The one or more non-transitory computer-readable media of claim 18, wherein generating the data representing the plurality of checkpoints comprises, for each record of the one or more records, determining the at least one value for the at least one other field based at least in part on performing an idempotent overwrite operation on one or more other values for the at least one other field associated with one or more other records of the datastore.
